retrieval_text: Bitter Almond is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 214 works and 614 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Bitter Almond, Bitter Almonds. Common plant-part co-mentions include Leaf, Seed, Bark, Fruit, Resin, Flower. Common preparation co-mentions include Oil, Essence, Powder, Syrup, Emetic, Ointment. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Epilepsy or convulsions, Vomiting or nausea, Cough, Fever, Wounds or ulcers. Caution co-mentions include Toxicity watch, Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Fatal-risk warning, Child-use caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include The elements of materia medica and therapeutics / by Jonathan Pereira.; The dispensatory of the United States of America / by Geo. B. Wood and Franklin Bache.; The elements of materia medica. Comprehending the natural history, preparation, properties, composition, effects and uses of medicines / [Jonathan Pereira].; The elements of materia medica : comprehending the natural history, preparation, properties, composition, effects and uses of medicines / by Jonathan Pereira, F.R.S. & L.S.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
